blurb: MeetingTV sued Palo Alto Networks and its recently acquired Koi Security, alleging Koi published AI-generated threat attribution falsely tying MeetingTV to Chinese cyber-espionage to inflate its valuation ahead of the $400M deal. The suit puts the price and diligence of the acquisition in question and raises procurement risk for enterprises weighing Palo Alto's Wings AI platform, which the complaint says produced unverified hallucinations without human review.

title: Meta reuses old RAM in new servers with custom bridge chip
blurb: Meta developed a custom CXL bridge chip called Vistara to reuse older DIMMs from decommissioned servers alongside native memory in new machines, addressing the roughly 40% of its servers that are memory-constrained. The workaround sidesteps soaring RAM prices, which are projected to double by the end of 2026 amid a shortage expected to persist into 2027, without the performance hit of plugging old memory directly into newer hardware.

title: Alibaba bans Claude Code after Anthropic is caught tracking Chinese users with hidden code
blurb: Alibaba banned employees from using Anthropic's Claude Code effective July 10, after researchers found steganographic tracking code that flagged Chinese users by timezone and proxy domain and hid the signals in prompts sent back to Anthropic's servers. Anthropic called it an anti-distillation experiment, but Alibaba classified the tool as high-risk software and is steering staff to its own Qoder coding agent instead.

title: EU appears to find datacenter emissions easier to offset than lobbyists
blurb: The EU is advancing rules to curb datacenter carbon emissions, but industry lobbying has stalled the reporting and efficiency mandates that would give the targets teeth. Enterprise operators running EU workloads face the prospect of new energy-reporting obligations and PUE thresholds that could raise compliance overhead and shape where hyperscalers and colocation providers site their next builds.

title: Outlook contacts will start appearing in Phone, Messages, Siri, and other apps on iOS
blurb: Microsoft is building a feature that lets iOS surface Outlook contacts natively across Phone, Messages, Siri, and other apps without users manually copying them into the local address book. The change deepens Outlook's integration on Apple devices and reduces friction for enterprises standardizing on Microsoft 365, keeping corporate contact data inside managed apps rather than duplicated across the device.

title: SK hynix, Samsung, Micron among semiconductor industry group lobbying against government intervention on domestic memory chip supply — says move would worsen situation, suggests tax deductions on consumer electronics instead
blurb: SEMI, the semiconductor industry group representing Micron, Samsung, and SK hynix, sent a letter to the Trump administration opposing any government intervention in the memory market, arguing that price or capacity controls would prolong the shortage now projected to last through 2027. As an alternative to Sen. Bernie Moreno's push for auto-industry supply guarantees, the group proposed tax deductions on consumer electronics to offset AI-driven price spikes.

title: US residents angry datacenters 'shoved down our throats' are recalling officials
blurb: Datacenter opposition is spreading across US municipalities, with Lenox Township, Michigan residents petitioning to recall four board trustees after emails revealed developers had privately courted officials for a project the township publicly denied existed. The bipartisan backlash over water, power, and property-value concerns adds local-permitting risk to hyperscaler and colocation expansion, meaning enterprise buyers planning capacity in rural markets should factor in moratoriums and reversible zoning approvals.

title: AI bills are baffling the C-suite after shift to usage-based pricing
blurb: Enterprise AI vendors' shift from per-seat licenses to usage-based, token-metered pricing has left finance and IT leaders unable to forecast or reconcile their AI bills, with consumption spiking unpredictably as employees and agents scale usage. The opacity complicates procurement and budgeting, pushing CIOs toward FinOps tooling and contract terms that cap or flatten spend before variable costs erode the ROI case for deploying AI at all.

title: Don’t waste your next cloud outage
blurb: The author argues that enterprises should treat cloud outages as a normal cost of doing business and use each one to harden architecture, citing a year of hyperscaler failures including Google Cloud's June 2025 internetwide disruption, repeated AWS US-East-1 incidents, and Azure's 10-hour storage outage in February 2026. The takeaway for IT leaders is that resilience, redundancy, and contingency planning are now strategic requirements, not technical afterthoughts, given how concentrated dependence on a few providers turns one vendor's failure into an industry-wide one.

title: What Meta, Oracle moves say about data center economics
blurb: Oracle's latest 10-K details the risks that its AI data center buildout, including the $300B Stargate contract with OpenAI, could cost more or run late, while Meta is reportedly planning a new business to sell excess compute capacity and recoup its infrastructure spend. The filings signal hyperscalers are shifting from a capacity land grab toward managing return and financial risk, as the top four plan up to $725B in AI infrastructure this year.

title: You can now sign into Microsoft Edge with a Google account
blurb: Microsoft added Google account sign-in to Edge, letting users sync bookmarks, passwords, and settings from Chrome without a manual import. The move lowers the switching cost for Chrome holdouts and is a direct play to pull users off Google's browser at a time when Edge is fighting to grow desktop share against Chrome's dominant install base.

title: Veeam leads 2026 Gartner Backup MQ by a whisker
blurb: Veeam edged ahead of Cohesity, Commvault, and Rubrik to top Gartner's 2026 Backup and Data Protection Platform Magic Quadrant, with the four vendors clustered tightly in the Leaders' box and Druva trailing behind them. Gartner also dropped Kaseya's Unitrends from the 11-vendor field and projected that 90% of backup platforms will embed generative AI for management and support by 2029, up from under 2% today.

title: Chip industry group warns US government against interventionist approach to memory chip shortage
blurb: SEMI, the chip industry trade group whose members include Micron, SK Hynix, and Samsung, wrote to senior Trump administration officials on July 1 urging them to stop influencing memory prices and production capacity, warning that intervention would distort markets and prolong the shortage. The group instead backed long-term purchase agreements and extended tax breaks to lift US capacity, as data centers are projected to consume up to 70% of global memory output in 2026.

title: Amazon Leo constellation nears 400 satellites as broadband launch looms
blurb: Amazon's Project Kuiper, now rebranded Leo, has roughly 400 satellites in orbit and is approaching the threshold needed to begin commercial broadband service. The buildout positions Amazon against SpaceX's Starlink for enterprise and government connectivity contracts, giving IT buyers a second low-earth-orbit provider to weigh for remote-site and backup links.

title: Unpacking Workday’s agentic AI pricing model
blurb: Workday moved to a hybrid pricing model that pairs core seat-based subscriptions with a pool of Flex Credits that customers spend on AI agents and other consumption-based platform features, with credit allotments scaled to company size. The shift ties cost to usage rather than headcount, and it lands as only 35% of CIOs report full visibility into their AI operating spend, per a KPMG survey. For buyers, each vendor's own metering scheme adds another layer to already-fragmented consumption budgeting, and the practical direction is up.

title: Google Workspace Weekly Recap - July 3, 2026
blurb: Google's Workspace recap centers on Gemini expanding across the suite, including single-prompt native presentation generation in Slides, Ask Gemini and AI Overviews arriving on Drive mobile, and Gemini Canvas sharing into Classroom. Admins also gained data-region controls for the Gemini app down to the organizational-unit level, plus OU-scoped mobile device management privileges and mobile support for delegated Gmail accounts.

title: How data sovereignty is changing cloud native infrastructure design
blurb: The CNCF blog argues that data sovereignty has shifted from a geography problem to a jurisdictional one, since laws like the U.S. CLOUD Act tie data access to corporate control rather than server location. Choosing a Frankfurt region doesn't shield a hyperscaler's data from its parent company's home laws. The author frames this as a resilience concern, not just privacy, and notes regulated European enterprises are increasingly assembling sovereign platforms from open-source Kubernetes components instead of buying sovereignty as a hyperscaler add-on.

title: Don’t waste your next cloud outage
blurb: Cloud outages are now routine, and the author argues each one is a resilience audit teams should exploit rather than wait out. A single hyperscaler failure in a control plane, identity system, or region now cascades across vendors, as recent US-East-1 and Azure Front Door incidents showed. The post's three prescriptions center on treating redundancy, contingency planning, and dependency mapping as strategic requirements, not post-incident afterthoughts.

title: PostgreSQL and the OOM killer: Why we use strict memory overcommit
blurb: Ubicloud's team explains why they run PostgreSQL under strict memory overcommit (vm.overcommit_memory=2), which forces malloc failures instead of letting the Linux OOM killer terminate a backend. Killing a single backend risks corrupting shared memory segments, so the postmaster responds by dropping every connection and forcing crash recovery. The post also covers a three-character kernel bug that made them temporarily disable the setting, plus their heuristic for tuning the overcommit ratio.

title: Meta reuses old RAM in new servers with custom bridge chip
blurb: Meta developed a custom CXL bridge chip called Vistara to decouple decommissioned DIMMs from their original memory channels, letting it reuse older RAM in new servers without the performance hit of plugging legacy modules directly into memory slots. The move targets the roughly 40% of Meta's millions of servers bottlenecked on memory, and lands as RAM prices are forecast to double by the end of 2026 amid a shortage expected to run through 2027.

title: 100 Most Expensive Keywords for Google Ads (July 2026)
blurb: Ahrefs published its July 2026 ranking of the 100 most expensive Google Ads keywords, with legal services dominating at 17.52% of the top 10,000 costliest terms, followed by ecommerce (3.85%) and education (2.58%). The list pairs each keyword with monthly search volume and CPC, giving demand-gen teams a benchmark for where auction competition and purchase intent push bids highest before they build out paid search targets.

title: TBM 428: Yes, Robot. Yes, Boss?
blurb: John Cutler argues that AI amplifies whatever direction an organization is already moving rather than changing it. It can remove real drag when a behavior is blocked by workflow friction, missing scaffolding, or clerical burden, but it will not fix problems rooted in low psychological safety, misaligned incentives, or fear, and may make them worse by automating the existing avoidance and metric-weaponizing. The practical read for PMs: AI accelerates a team on the cusp of improvement and accelerates dysfunction just as easily.

title: Securitize tokenizes $295 million of its own stock on Solana and Avalanche amid NYSE debut
blurb: Securitize tokenized $295 million of its NYSE-listed shares (SECZ) on Solana and Avalanche on its first day of public trading, making it the largest issuer-sponsored tokenized stock at launch. The tokens represent the same common shares trading on the NYSE rather than a separate class, positioning issuer-run tokenization against third-party stock token products as Citi projects the tokenized-securities market could reach $5.5T by 2030.

title: Spotify asks Kalshi, Polymarket to remove branding after manipulated streams used to settle music bets: Bloomberg
blurb: Spotify asked Kalshi and Polymarket to strip its branding after finding manipulated streaming activity was used to settle prediction-market bets, Bloomberg reported. The platform removed more than 500,000 artificial streams that pushed Malcolm Todd's "Earrings" up its charts, a metric that had already resolved a Kalshi market drawing $3M in trading.

title: IMF says policy choices will determine whether tokenization strengthens or fragments the financial system
blurb: The IMF's Tobias Adrian argued in a blog post that policy decisions on money, market infrastructure, and legal frameworks will determine whether tokenization strengthens or fragments the financial system as assets move onto shared ledgers. He warned the shift could concentrate risk in platforms, code, and infrastructure providers rather than intermediary balance sheets, and identified tokenized deposits, stablecoins, and tokenized central bank reserves as the three emerging settlement assets.

title: Bitcoin whales bought $16.7 billion of bitcoin in 2 weeks even as ETFs bled a record $4 billion
blurb: Bitcoin whales accumulated more than 270,000 BTC ($16.7B) over the past two weeks even as US spot bitcoin ETFs shed a record $4.06B in June, their worst month since listing and enough to push the funds negative for 2026. The simultaneous split of institutions selling into large-holder accumulation is the divergence that has preceded past cycle lows.

title: US Treasury sanctions over 100 ISIS-K crypto addresses that moved over $1.4 million
blurb: The U.S. Treasury's OFAC sanctioned 134 crypto addresses tied to ISIS-K, including 131 Tron wallets that received more than $1.4 million since 2023, prompting Tether to freeze the balances in all 131 addresses. The action, which also hit a Brazil-linked network that laundered over $30 million for the PCC gang, underscores how centralized stablecoin issuers have become a direct enforcement lever for US sanctions.

title: Ondo Finance debuts SEC-aligned tokenized stock model with BlackRock ETF, Micron shares
blurb: Ondo Finance launched the first production deployment of the SEC's third-party custodial tokenization model, issuing BlackRock's IVV ETF and Micron shares as tokens on Ethereum through its SEC-registered transfer agent Oasis Pro. The structure keeps tokenized securities inside existing US market rules rather than offshore, though the product is not yet available to US investors.

title: ‘Markets find their footing’: Bitcoin holds $61,000 rebound ahead of US Independence Day as soft jobs data eases rate fears
blurb: Bitcoin held above $61,000 and ether above $1,700 on Friday, extending a rebound sparked by a soft June jobs report that showed payrolls growing 57,000 against roughly 114,000 expected, cutting the implied odds of a year-end Fed hike to 77% from 85%. US spot bitcoin ETFs snapped a 10-day outflow streak with $222 million in Thursday inflows, their first positive print after roughly $2.4 billion of June redemptions.

title: SBI Crypto to shut down mining pool that holds roughly 2% of Bitcoin's hashrate
blurb: SBI Crypto will shut down its Bitcoin mining pool on July 31, ending a service that accounts for roughly 2% of the network's total hashrate and giving miners under a month to redirect. The firm disclosed no reason for the closure, which lands as mining margins compress under a falling bitcoin price, declining hashrate, and rising operational costs.

title: Trump says there is ‘nothing wrong’ with family’s crypto windfall
blurb: President Trump defended the more than $1.4 billion he earned from crypto ventures in 2025, telling CNBC there was "nothing wrong" or illegal about the income and that he wants the US to lead in digital assets. Federal disclosures tied roughly $636 million to his memecoin, $594 million to World Liberty Financial, and $197 million to a stablecoin venture, making him the largest crypto earner in US politics.

title: Three years after MiCA became law, Europe's crypto framework is undergoing a rethink
blurb: The European Union opened a consultation on "MiCA 2.0," a review of its three-year-old crypto framework that will run through September and aims to broaden rules built for spot crypto to cover stablecoins and tokenized assets. The rethink follows the US GENIUS Act, with ECB officials softening their stance to tolerate stablecoins on bank balance sheets while still opposing their use in wholesale settlement.

title: Bank of Russia governor says ‘everything is ready’ for widespread use of digital ruble ahead of September rollout
blurb: Bank of Russia Governor Elvira Nabiullina said systemically important banks and large retailers are technologically ready for the digital ruble's nationwide rollout, with those banks set to offer the CBDC to clients starting September 1. The central bank is separately weighing stablecoins for international settlements as a complement to the digital ruble, while a broader crypto regulation bill has slipped past its original July 1 target.

title: Finally. $221 million flow into Bitcoin ETFs, ending a painful 10-day outflow streak
blurb: US-listed spot bitcoin ETFs pulled in $221.7 million on Thursday, their largest daily intake in two months and the end of a 10-day, $2.73 billion outflow streak, with Fidelity's FBTC leading at $166 million while BlackRock's IBIT bled $40.43 million. Year-to-date net outflows still sit near $5.4 billion, so analysts say a sustained inflow trend is needed to confirm a durable recovery.

title: Tokenization could make finance faster, but also more susceptible to shocks, IMF says
blurb: The IMF warned that tokenization, which moves stocks, bonds, and bank deposits onto shared blockchain ledgers, could speed settlement from days to seconds while removing the time buffers that let banks and regulators catch problems before they spread. Without updated regulation, the fund said the technology could amplify systemic risk, concentration, and volatile cross-border flows, particularly in emerging economies.

title: Binance says MiCA should be judged by who it licenses, not who it excludes
blurb: Binance withdrew its MiCA license application in Greece days before the July 1 deadline, halting some services and new EU registrations after ESMA privately advised national regulators to reject its applications. Europe head Gillian Lynch argued MiCA should be judged by how many firms it licenses rather than excludes, defended the exchange's financial-crime controls, and said Binance will pursue a new license and stay in the region.

title: EToro invests in onchain derivatives platform Extended as brokers race into DeFi
blurb: EToro led a $12.5M funding round for Extended, an onchain perpetual futures exchange founded by former Revolut staff, with Jump Crypto and Alber Blanc participating. The broker plans to embed Extended's perps engine into its recently acquired Zengo self-custody wallet, part of a broader push to bring DeFi products onchain as Robinhood expands its own tokenized-stock and perpetual futures offerings.

title: CryptoQuant says bitcoin and altcoin exchange deposits have spiked, indicating higher volatility ahead
blurb: CryptoQuant reported that bitcoin, ether, and altcoin deposits to exchanges have spiked, with BTC inflows reaching nearly 49,000 on June 30, a level hit only four other times this year and historically followed by sharp volatility. Average deposit size doubled to about 2 BTC, signaling that whales and institutions are repositioning as bitcoin trades near $62,180 and tests $60,000 support.

title: This sanctioned Russian stablecoin claims it processes billions, but blockchain analysts disagree
blurb: A7A5, a sanctioned ruble-backed stablecoin built to move value outside Western financial channels, says it averages roughly $205M in daily volume and processed $34.4B in the first half of 2026, mostly through DeFi. Blockchain analytics firms including TRM Labs and Elliptic dispute that, pegging daily volume near $75M and estimating that about 34% of it is circular transactions inflating the total.

title: Ondo Finance debuts SEC-aligned tokenized stock model with BlackRock ETF, Micron shares
blurb: Ondo Finance launched the first production deployment of the SEC's third-party custodial tokenization model, issuing BlackRock's IVV ETF and Micron shares as tokens on Ethereum through its SEC-registered transfer agent Oasis Pro, with Broadridge handling proxy voting and shareholder communications. The structure keeps tokenized securities inside existing US market rules rather than an offshore wrapper, though the product is not yet available to US investors.

title: Securitize tokenizes $295 million of its own stock on Solana and Avalanche amid NYSE debut
blurb: Securitize launched tokenized versions of its NYSE-listed shares on Solana and Avalanche on its first day as a public company, with investors holding roughly $295M in tokens, the largest issuer-sponsored tokenized stock at launch. The onchain shares represent the same SECZ common stock trading on the NYSE, not a separate class, positioning issuer-sponsored tokenization against third-party stock-token rivals as public equities move onchain.

title: Bitcoin whales bought $16.7 billion of bitcoin in 2 weeks even as ETFs bled a record $4 billion
blurb: Bitcoin whales accumulated more than 270,000 BTC ($16.7B) over two weeks while U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs shed a record $4.06B in June, their worst month since listing and enough to push the funds negative for 2026. The split of institutions selling into large holders buying has historically appeared near cycle lows, with long-term holders absorbing coins before any price recovery.

title: Spotify asks Kalshi, Polymarket to remove branding after manipulated streams used to settle music bets: Bloomberg
blurb: Spotify asked Kalshi and Polymarket to remove its logo and clarify that neither operates a partnership, after users manipulated its song rankings to win prediction market bets. The streaming service scrubbed more than 500,000 artificial streams that had lifted Malcolm Todd's "Earrings" up its charts, but only after those inflated figures settled a Kalshi market that drew $3 million in trading.

title: US Treasury sanctions over 100 ISIS-K crypto addresses that moved over $1.4 million
blurb: The US Treasury's OFAC sanctioned 134 crypto addresses tied to ISIS-K, including 131 Tron wallets that received over $1.4 million since 2023, and Tether promptly froze the balances in all 131 accounts. The action underscores how centralized stablecoin issuers have become de facto enforcement arms, with Tether now freezing sanctioned funds on-chain far faster than traditional banks can process a settlement hold.

title: IMF says policy choices will determine whether tokenization strengthens or fragments the financial system
blurb: The IMF's Tobias Adrian said policy decisions on money, market infrastructure, and legal frameworks will determine whether tokenization strengthens or fragments the financial system as assets migrate onto shared ledgers. The IMF flagged that tokenization could concentrate risk in platforms, code, and infrastructure providers rather than intermediary balance sheets, and identified three emerging settlement assets: tokenized bank deposits, stablecoins, and tokenized central bank reserves.

title: ‘Markets find their footing’: Bitcoin holds $61,000 rebound ahead of US Independence Day as soft jobs data eases rate fears
blurb: Bitcoin held above $61,000 and ether above $1,700 on Friday, extending a rebound sparked by a soft June jobs report that showed 57,000 payrolls added against roughly 114,000 expected, cooling bets on further Fed tightening. U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs snapped a 10-day outflow streak with $224 million in Thursday inflows, an early sign dip buyers are returning after roughly $2.4 billion of June redemptions.

title: SBI Crypto to shut down mining pool that holds roughly 2% of Bitcoin's hashrate
blurb: SBI Crypto will shut down its Bitcoin mining pool on July 31, ending a service that accounts for roughly 2% of the network's hashrate and giving miners under a month to redirect their contributions elsewhere. The firm, part of Japanese financial conglomerate SBI Group, disclosed no reason for the closure, which lands as mining margins compress and hashrate slides from its October high with some operators pivoting to AI infrastructure.

title: Three years after MiCA became law, Europe's crypto framework is undergoing a rethink
blurb: The EU opened a consultation on "MiCA 2.0," a review of its crypto framework that closes around September, aiming to rework rules originally built for spot crypto to address stablecoins and tokenization now dominating institutional and wholesale finance. European policymakers, including at the ECB, have softened their opposition to stablecoins since the US passed the GENIUS Act, though they remain wary of using them for wholesale settlement.

title: Trump says there is ‘nothing wrong’ with family’s crypto windfall
blurb: President Trump defended the more than $1.4 billion his family earned from crypto ventures in 2025, telling CNBC there was "nothing wrong" or illegal about the income even as his administration writes the industry's rules. Federal disclosures tie roughly $636 million to his memecoin, $594 million to World Liberty Financial, and $197 million to a stablecoin venture, making him the largest crypto earner in U.S. politics.

title: Binance says MiCA should be judged by who it licenses, not who it excludes
blurb: Binance withdrew its MiCA license application in Greece just days before the July 1 deadline, suspending some services and new registrations for EU users after ESMA privately advised national regulators to reject it. Europe head Gillian Lynch argued the regime should be judged by how many firms it brings inside the regulated market, and said Binance remains committed to securing a new EU license.

title: Finally. $221 million flow into Bitcoin ETFs, ending a painful 10-day outflow streak
blurb: U.S.-listed spot bitcoin ETFs pulled in $221.7 million on Thursday, their largest daily intake in two months, ending a 10-day outflow streak that had drained $2.73 billion. Fidelity's FBTC led with $166 million while BlackRock's IBIT logged a $40.43 million outflow, but year-to-date net outflows still sit near $5.4 billion, and analysts say a sustained inflow trend is needed to confirm any lasting recovery.

title: Bank of Russia governor says ‘everything is ready’ for widespread use of digital ruble ahead of September rollout
blurb: Bank of Russia Governor Elvira Nabiullina said systemically important banks and large retailers are technologically ready for the digital ruble's nationwide rollout, with those banks set to offer the CBDC to clients starting September 1. The central bank is separately weighing stablecoins for international settlements as a complement to the digital ruble, while a broader crypto-regulation bill remains stalled in parliament.

title: EU Agrees to Cut ESG Reporting Requirements for Asset Managers
blurb: The EU agreed to scale back ESG disclosure rules for asset managers, dropping the requirement to report sustainability data across every asset they hold under revised regulations. The rollback eases compliance costs for European money managers and signals a broader retreat from the bloc's aggressive sustainable-finance reporting regime.

title: Tokenization could make finance faster, but also more susceptible to shocks, IMF says
blurb: The IMF warned that tokenization, which represents assets like stocks, bonds, and bank deposits on shared ledgers, can settle trades in seconds but also strips out the clearing and reconciliation delays that let banks and regulators catch problems before they spread. Without updated regulation, the IMF's Tobias Adrian argued, tokenization could amplify systemic risk, concentration, and volatile cross-border flows, especially in emerging economies.

title: Microsoft Disclosure Provides Rare Glimpse of Tax Haven Tactics
blurb: Microsoft's disclosure of its European operations revealed how the company routed profits through low-tax jurisdictions, offering a rare public look at tax-optimization structures that most multinationals keep private. A new EU directive will soon force other U.S. companies to file similar country-by-country reports, exposing effective tax rates and profit-shifting arrangements that regulators and competitors have long been unable to see.

title: Starling Bank to axe 130 jobs
blurb: Starling Bank confirmed it will cut around 130 roles across its banking and technology units, restructuring to reduce duplication and speed product delivery as it leans harder on AI. The move follows a 3% drop in pre-tax profit to £217m and revenue falling to £887m, even as the challenger bank keeps hiring engineers and rolls out its Starling Assistant agentic AI tool.

title: CPP Invests $1.75 Billion in EQT’s AI Buildout
blurb: Canada Pension Plan Investment Board committed $1.75B to back EQT AB's AI infrastructure buildout, deepening pension-capital exposure to the data-center and compute assets underpinning the AI boom. The deal signals large institutional allocators are moving past public-market AI bets into direct infrastructure financing, where private-equity managers like EQT can deploy long-duration capital against multiyear buildout timelines.

title: Stretch Marks
blurb: Strategy has bought 174,863 Bitcoin for $13.7B this year, roughly 70% of net digital-asset flows, funding the purchases with Stretch, a preferred security that pays a variable dividend engineered to hold a $100 par. The essay argues this credit structure may not be sustainable: the company now owns 4% of total Bitcoin supply, and its $10.5B of STRC leaves it exposed if it can no longer keep issuing to fund fresh buying into a market down 30% this year.

title: This sanctioned Russian stablecoin claims it processes billions, but blockchain analysts disagree
blurb: A7A5, a sanctioned Russian ruble-pegged stablecoin built to move value outside Western financial channels, claims $205M in average daily volume and $34.4B processed this year, while analytics firms including TRM Labs and Elliptic peg real activity far lower and heavily inflated by circular transfers. TRM Labs puts daily volume closer to $75M, estimating 34% of it as wash-like circular movement tied to the token's issuer.
